data.title: Ethereum Foundation Opens Applications for Protocol Fellowship Cohort 7
data.description:
The Ethereum Foundation has announced that applications for the Ethereum Protocol Fellowship Cohort 7 (EPF7) are now open, with a deadline of May 13. According to Odaily, the program aims to train engineers to participate in the development of Ethereum's core protocol, focusing on key network attributes such as censorship resistance, open-source nature, privacy, and security. It will emphasize client implementation, protocol specification, testing, and cutting-edge research.

EPF7 will adopt a 'small-scale, high-density' model, reducing the number of participants to enhance mentorship depth and project contribution quality. The program will run from June to November, offering selected participants guidance from the Ethereum core developer community. Some participants will receive monthly stipends to focus on protocol development work. The project aims to deliver long-term contributors to the Ethereum core research team and foster substantial achievements in client development and protocol research.

The EPF team will host an online briefing on May 6 at 15:00 UTC to further explain project details and answer application-related questions.

data.title: France's Finance Ministry Maintains Growth Forecast for 2026
data.description: France's Finance Ministry has stated that there is no reason to revise its growth forecast. According to Jin10, the government projects a GDP growth of 0.9% for 2026.

data.title: Trump Administration Withdraws Nomination for U.S. Surgeon General
data.description: The Trump administration has decided to withdraw the nomination of wellness entrepreneur Casey Means for the position of U.S. Surgeon General, as confirmed by a White House official. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the unexpected move by the administration. The decision comes amid ongoing discussions and evaluations within the government regarding key health positions. Casey Means, known for her work in the wellness industry, was initially considered for the role to bring a fresh perspective to public health leadership. The withdrawal of her nomination marks a significant shift in the administration's approach to filling this critical position. Further details on the reasons behind this decision have not been disclosed by the White House.

data.title: US-Iran Tensions Impact Energy Prices and Investor Concerns
data.description: The ongoing standoff between the United States and Iran is maintaining energy prices at levels that pose a threat to the global economy. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the concerns investors face as they assess potential risks that could adversely affect their portfolios. The geopolitical tension adds to the complexity of market analysis, with energy prices being a critical factor in economic stability. Investors are advised to remain vigilant as they navigate these uncertain times.

data.title: USDT0 Holder Distribution Reveals Concentration Among Small Investors
data.description: According to NS3.AI, recent data on USDT0 holders indicates that 99.2% of them possess less than $1,000 in holdings. This group comprises approximately 1,200 holders. In contrast, there are 35 holders with assets ranging from $100,000 to $1 million, and another 35 holders with more than $10 million.

data.title: The Ethereum Foundation (EPF7) is now open for applications, with a deadline of May 13th.
data.description: The Ethereum Foundation has announced that the Ethereum Protocol Fellowship Cohort 7 (EPF7) is now open for applications, with a deadline of May 13th. This program aims to cultivate engineers capable of participating in the development of the Ethereum core protocol, focusing on core network attributes including censorship resistance, open source nature, privacy, and security. Key areas of focus include client implementation, protocol specification, testing, and cutting-edge research. This cohort of EPF7 will adopt a "small-scale, high-density" model, reducing the number of participants to enhance the depth of mentorship and the quality of project contributions, while strengthening collaboration opportunities with the core development team. The program will run from June to November. Selected participants will receive mentorship and support from the Ethereum core developer community, and some will also receive monthly grants to focus on protocol development. The program's goals include cultivating long-term contributors to the Ethereum core R&D team and driving participants to achieve substantial results in client development and protocol research. The EPF team will hold an online briefing on May 6th at 15:00 UTC to further explain the project details and answer application-related questions.

data.title: MegaETH's MEGA Token Experiences Significant Drop on First Trading Day
data.description: MegaETH's MEGA token experienced a substantial decline of approximately 55% on its initial trading day, settling around $0.17 after briefly reaching $0.38 shortly after trading commenced. According to NS3.AI, the project distributed around 500 million tokens to participants of the October public sale. Despite the drop, the token maintained a trading price above the $0.0999 clearing price from the sale.

data.title: Marex Analyst: XRP Faces Macro Pressure and Weak Spot Flow
data.description: XRP continues to trade significantly below its record high, according to Marex analyst Louis De Backer. The cryptocurrency is currently 62% below its peak value, primarily due to macroeconomic pressures and weak spot flow, which are hindering standalone demand. According to NS3.AI, Polymarket bettors are predicting a 61% probability that XRP will reach $1 before January 1. Additionally, there is a 13% chance that XRP could hit $3.60 before the end of the year.

data.title: U.S. Air Force to Acquire Drones from Trump-Linked Company
data.description:
The U.S. Air Force has agreed to purchase a batch of interceptor drones from a company supported by U.S. President Donald Trump's son, though the quantity remains unspecified. According to Odaily, this decision comes as the U.S.-Iran conflict enters its third month, further strengthening ties between the U.S. military and defense contractors associated with the Trump family.

Powerus co-founder Velikovich stated that the company will sell these drones to the Pentagon following a demonstration in Arizona. This marks Powerus's first contract to supply such weapons to the U.S. military. The company has not disclosed the terms or scale of the deal, but such transactions are common when the military evaluates new weapon systems. The move aligns with the U.S. strategy to counter Iranian attack drones using cost-effective interceptor drones instead of expensive missiles. Reports indicate that the U.S. military has already deployed 10,000 AI-equipped Merops interceptor drones, developed in Ukraine, to the Middle East.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Senate Committee Supports Legislation to Restrict Minors' Access to AI Chatbots
data.description: A powerful Senate committee has endorsed legislation aimed at preventing minors from using chatbots developed by companies like OpenAI and Meta. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing public concern over the potential harm AI poses to children and teenagers. The proposed legislation reflects increasing scrutiny and calls for regulation in the AI industry to safeguard younger users from potential risks associated with artificial intelligence technologies.

data.title: Powerus, a drone company backed by the Trump family, signed a weapons procurement agreement with the U.S. military.
data.description: The U.S. Air Force has agreed to purchase an undisclosed number of interceptor drones from a company backed by President Trump's son. This move deepens the ties between the U.S. military and defense contractors affiliated with the Trump family as the war enters its third month. Powerus co-founder Veljkovic stated that the company will sell the drones to the Pentagon after a demonstration in Arizona, marking Powerus's first contract to sell such weapons to the U.S. military. The company declined to disclose the terms or size, but such deals are common for the military when evaluating new weapon systems. This move aligns with the U.S. strategy of countering Iranian attack drones with inexpensive interceptor drones, rather than expensive missiles. Reports indicate that the U.S. military has already deployed 10,000 AI-powered Merops interceptor drones, developed in Ukraine, to the Middle East. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Megaether Token Distribution: 50% of Holders Retain MEGA
data.description: According to Bubblemaps, during Megaether's token generation event on April 30, a total of 8,360 addresses received MEGA tokens. According to NS3.AI, half of these addresses have retained their tokens, while 40% have sold their entire allocation. Additionally, 10% of the addresses have partially sold their holdings.

data.title: Elon Musk Criticizes Cryptocurrency Amid OpenAI Lawsuit
data.description:
Elon Musk, during a court hearing related to his lawsuit with OpenAI, stated that while some cryptocurrencies hold value, the majority are scams. According to Odaily, this comment was made during cross-examination at the Oakland, California court, where the case involved OpenAI's early consideration of raising funds through an Initial Coin Offering (ICO).

Reports indicate that Musk highlighted the speculative and fraudulent activities prevalent in the crypto asset sector, drawing significant market attention.

Musk's stance on the cryptocurrency market has been notably volatile. In 2021, during the bull market, he influenced Tesla to purchase approximately $1.5 billion in Bitcoin and frequently mentioned Dogecoin, causing significant price surges in related assets. However, in 2022, Tesla reduced its Bitcoin holdings by 75% and recorded an impairment of about $222 million on the remaining holdings in the first quarter of 2026. As per the latest financial report, Tesla still holds approximately 11,509 Bitcoins, with a book value of around $786 million.

data.title: Benchmark strongly supports the Strategy STRC model: it's not a circular funding mechanism, but a long-term funding engine for Bitcoin.
data.description: The debate surrounding Strategy's financing model of continuously increasing its Bitcoin holdings through preferred stock STRC continues to widen. In a recent report, Benchmark analyst Mark Palmer stated that the interpretation of the STRC model as a "circular financing or Ponzi scheme" is a "serious misinterpretation," emphasizing that the mechanism is an "intentionally designed and sustainable capital framework" whose core logic is to translate market demand for returns into long-term Bitcoin exposure. According to SEC 8-K filings, Strategy raised approximately $3.5 billion in the first three weeks of April, with over 85% coming from STRC issuance. In the following three weeks, it purchased a total of 51,364 Bitcoins in three separate transactions, worth approximately $3.9 billion. Currently, Strategy's Bitcoin holdings have increased to 818,334, with a market capitalization of approximately $62.5 billion, and have recently returned to a floating profit of approximately $700 million. Benchmark believes that this structure does not rely on continuous issuance to operate and could even pay preferred stock dividends if necessary by selling a portion of the Bitcoin. However, significant skepticism remains in the market, with some arguing that selling off leveraged assets to pay dividends could be seen as a risk signal, triggering broader market pressure. (The Block)

data.title: White House Considers Increasing U.S. Oil Production
data.description: The White House National Economic Council Director, Kevin Hassett, announced plans to potentially increase U.S. oil production. According to Odaily, Hassett emphasized ongoing communication with oil companies to explore ways to boost domestic production.

data.title: Kelp Completes Funding Commitment to DeFi United Recovery Fund
data.description:
Kelp has announced the completion of its funding commitment to the 'DeFi United' recovery fund, contributing a total of 2,000 ETH. According to Odaily, this investment aims to facilitate the restoration of rsETH collateral assets and the normalization of the system.

Kelp stated that the contribution is a one-time investment, directly allocated to restoring the nominal exchange rate of rsETH with its underlying assets. It will also support the comprehensive repair of the system, including recapitalizing bridge vaults, restoring oracle functions, and addressing funding gaps in affected markets. Kelp emphasized that prior to the public announcement, it had assured rsETH holders that they would not incur losses, and this action is a direct fulfillment of that promise. As funds from various parties are gradually secured, the rsETH collateral structure will be restored to normal operation in phases.

data.title: ECB Likely to Raise Interest Rates Amid Energy Concerns
data.description: European Central Bank (ECB) policymakers are expected to increase interest rates at their upcoming meeting in June. Bloomberg posted on X that this decision hinges on the absence of positive developments regarding energy prices and the resolution of the Iran war. The ECB's stance reflects ongoing concerns about inflationary pressures driven by energy costs and geopolitical tensions. The central bank aims to address these challenges through monetary policy adjustments, with the potential rate hike being a key measure under consideration.

data.title: Elon Musk's claim during the OpenAI lawsuit trial that "most cryptocurrencies are scams" has once again drawn market attention.
data.description: During his lawsuit against OpenAI, Elon Musk stated that "some cryptocurrencies have value, but most are scams." This statement came during cross-examination in an Oakland, California court, when the case concerned OpenAI's early plans to raise funds through an ICO (Initial Coin Offering). Musk reportedly pointed out in court that there is a lot of speculation and fraud in the crypto asset space, drawing market attention. It's worth noting that Musk's attitude towards the crypto market has fluctuated significantly over the years: during the 2021 bull market, he pushed Tesla to purchase approximately $1.5 billion worth of Bitcoin and repeatedly mentioned Dogecoin publicly, driving a significant increase in the price of related assets. However, Tesla reduced its Bitcoin holdings by 75% in 2022 and recorded an impairment loss of approximately $222 million on the remaining holdings in the first quarter of 2026. As of the latest financial report, it still holds approximately 11,509 Bitcoins, with a book value of approximately $786 million. (Fortune)

data.title: U.S. Senator Criticizes Trump's War Strategy
data.description: A leading Democratic member of the U.S. Senate Armed Services Committee has criticized U.S. President Donald Trump for lacking a coherent strategy on war-related issues. According to Odaily, the senator expressed concerns that the current strategic situation of the United States is worse than before the outbreak of war. The senator also noted that the Iranian regime continues to pose a threat to American interests.

data.title: Trump Administration Official Suggests Private Lenders Aid Spirit Airlines
data.description: A senior official from the Trump administration has indicated that private lenders could play a crucial role in supporting Spirit Airlines as it faces operational challenges. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that these lenders might assist the airline while discussions for a potential rescue package from the U.S. government are underway. Spirit Airlines has been experiencing financial difficulties, prompting the need for external support to maintain its operations. The involvement of private lenders could provide the necessary relief as negotiations continue.
